sections.instruction: Questions 11 and 12: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 13 and 14: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 15-20: What does the manager say about each of the following aspects of the company policy for apprentices? Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 15-20.

questions.content: Which TWO pieces of advice for the first week of an apprenticeship does the manager give?
Which TWO things does the manager say mentors can help with?

options: C It is against the rules.

sections.instruction: Questions 21-25: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 26-30: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 26-30.

questions.content: Carla and Rob were surprised to learn that coastal cities
options: A contain nearly half the world’s population.
B include most of the world’s largest cities.
C are growing twice as fast as other cities.

questions.content: According to Rob, building coastal cities near to rivers
options: A may bring pollution to the cities.
B may reduce the land available for agriculture.
C may mean the countryside is spoiled by industry.

questions.content: What mistake was made when building water drainage channels in Miami in the 1950s?
options: A There were not enough of them.
B They were made of unsuitable materials.
C They did not allow for the effects of climate change.

questions.content: What do Rob and Carla think that the authorities in Miami should do immediately?
options: A take measures to restore ecosystems
B pay for a new flood prevention system
C stop disposing of waste materials into the ocean

questions.content: What do they agree should be the priority for international action?

options: D check the information is accurate

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Marine renewable energy (ocean energy)
Introduction
More energy required because of growth in population and (31) {{BLANK}}
What’s needed:
• renewable energy sources
• methods that won’t create pollution
Wave energy
Advantage: waves provide a (32) {{BLANK}} source of renewable energy
Electricity can be generated using offshore or onshore systems
Onshore systems may use a reservoir
Problems:
• waves can move in any (33) {{BLANK}}
• movement of sand, etc. on the (34) {{BLANK}} of the ocean may be affected
Tidal energy
Tides are more (35) {{BLANK}} than waves
Planned tidal lagoon in Wales:
• will be created in a (36) {{BLANK}} at Swansea
• breakwater (dam) containing 16 turbines
• rising tide forces water through turbines, generating electricity
• stored water is released through (37) {{BLANK}}, driving the turbines in the reverse direction
Advantages:
• not dependent on weather
• no (38) {{BLANK}} is required to make it work
• likely to create a number of (39) {{BLANK}}
Problem:
• may harm fish and birds, e.g. by affecting (40) {{BLANK}} and building up silt
Ocean thermal energy conversion
Uses a difference in temperature between the surface and lower levels
Water brought to the surface in a pipe

questions.content:
Marine renewable energy (ocean energy)
Introduction
More energy required because of growth in population and (31) {{BLANK}}
What’s needed:
• renewable energy sources
• methods that won’t create pollution
Wave energy
Advantage: waves provide a (32) {{BLANK}} source of renewable energy
Electricity can be generated using offshore or onshore systems
Onshore systems may use a reservoir
Problems:
• waves can move in any (33) {{BLANK}}
• movement of sand, etc. on the (34) {{BLANK}} of the ocean may be affected
Tidal energy
Tides are more (35) {{BLANK}} than waves
Planned tidal lagoon in Wales:
• will be created in a (36) {{BLANK}} at Swansea
• breakwater (dam) containing 16 turbines
• rising tide forces water through turbines, generating electricity
• stored water is released through (37) {{BLANK}}, driving the turbines in the reverse direction
Advantages:
• not dependent on weather
• no (38) {{BLANK}} is required to make it work
• likely to create a number of (39) {{BLANK}}
Problem:
• may harm fish and birds, e.g. by affecting (40) {{BLANK}} and building up silt
Ocean thermal energy conversion
Uses a difference in temperature between the surface and lower levels
Water brought to the surface in a pipe
